summary: Large item-system decompilation and typing pass. The PR added or rewrote substantial portions of item core code, especially src/melee/it/it_2725.c, src/melee/it/it_266F.c, and a new src/melee/it/itcoll.c, while expanding item headers, item variable structs, item logic/data tables, and function prototypes across item, fighter, stage, game-mode, collision, and baselib headers. It also implemented more complete Chicorita and Chicorita leaf item behavior and adjusted many item-specific headers/call sites to use stronger Item_GObj/HSD_GObj/void pointer types. Reviewer approved the work, noting only uncertainty about why it_3F14.c had to be commented out; the author later said the item tables were intentionally consolidated into one file for now because the best file split was unclear.

key_files.why: Largest changed file; added broad item includes, large item logic/GX link tables such as it_803F1418 and it_803F14C4, and substantial item core implementation work.
New item collision implementation file with many item collision routines and mpColl integration.
Major item core rewrite with stronger types, static data, spawn/selection logic, helper inlines, and collision/state callbacks.
Entire existing file was commented out in the diff; reviewer specifically flagged uncertainty about why this was necessary.
Expanded item-specific variable and attribute structs including Klap, Chicorita, Chicorita leaf, it_279D, it_27B5, and corrected offsets/Vec3 layout for existing vars.
Large item type/layout changes supporting the decompilation and new item vars.
Added ft/forward.h dependency and introduced ItCmd typedef as void (*ItCmd)(Item_GObj*, FtCmdState*).
Header updated alongside the item core implementation with many corrected item prototypes.
Implemented/expanded Chicorita behavior, timer/y-velocity item vars, and leaf spawning links.
Implemented/expanded Chicorita leaf spawning and behavior using itChicoritaLeafAttr.
Added Struct2074 and replaced a raw/int padding region in Fighter with a typed struct; comment notes possible analogous item layout cleanup for item->xD94 through xDA8.
Added/annotated MPCOLL flag constants, including MPCOLL_FLAGS_B20 and MPCOLL_FLAGS_B21.
Added baselib JObj definitions needed by item work.

what_changed: Added a new src/melee/it/itcoll.c with item collision code and declared related APIs in src/melee/it/itcoll.h, including functions such as itColl_BounceOffVictim and itColl_BounceOffShield.
Greatly expanded src/melee/it/it_2725.c, adding many item module includes and consolidating large item data/logic tables into this file, including it_803F1418 and it_803F14C4.

Reworked src/melee/it/it_266F.c with stronger item typing, static vectors/quaternions/data, HSD object allocator declarations, helper inlines, and rewritten item spawn/selection/physics/collision routines.
Commented out the contents of src/melee/it/it_3F14.c rather than deleting it; the review explicitly says this was not understood but could be fixed later.

Expanded item common variable/attribute structs in itCommonItems.h, including itKlap_ItemVars, itChicorita_ItemVars, itChicoritaAttr, itChicoritaLeafAttr, it_279D_ItemVars, it_279D_DatAttrs, it_27B5_ItemVars, it_27B5_DatAttrs, and it_27CE_ItemVars.
Changed itKinoko_ItemVars to store x4 as a Vec3 instead of separate scalar fields and updated DK mushroom/kinoko call sites to access x4.x and x4.y.
Added Chicorita and Chicorita leaf behavior: Chicorita initializes timer/y-velocity vars, uses attr values for lifetime/timing, and leaf spawning uses parent Chicorita position/facing/attributes.

Corrected many function prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or loose scalar types to concrete return and parameter types, for example ft_80089228 returns float, ft_80089768 takes Vec2*, Stage_80224FDC returns bool, gm_80165084 returns bool, gm_8016C6C0 takes Item_GObj*, and gm_801BEC54 returns void*.
Introduced ItCmd in it/forward.h as a function pointer taking Item_GObj* and FtCmdState*, requiring ft/forward.h.
Adjusted fighter collision/lib prototypes and implementations to accept void* where item or generic victim data was needed, such as ftColl_80076808 victim and ftLib_800874CC arg1.
Added/renamed map-collision flag definitions in common_structs.h, including B20/B21 and a comment questioning MPCOLL_UNK as floor-related.
Changed grMaterial item script access from item->x52C_item_script to item->x524_cmd.x8, indicating a refined Item command/script struct layout.
Added Struct2074 to fighter types and replaced raw Fighter x2074 padding with a typed Vec2/Vec2/u32/u16 struct, with a TODO suggesting similar treatment for item fields xD94 through xDA8.
smart_moves: The PR reduced uncertainty by replacing many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures, letting downstream item code compile against better type information.
Centralizing uncertain item tables in it_2725.c was an explicit pragmatic choice by the author while the correct long-term file split was unknown.
Using typed Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, Fighter, Vec2, Vec3, and struct fields instead of raw ints/padding exposed layout relationships needed by item decompilation.
Adding item variable structs in itCommonItems.h made item-specific xDD4 overlays searchable and reusable across individual item files.
The change used offset comments in structs such as ip+DD4/ip+E34 and fp+2074, preserving decomp-critical layout evidence while names remained provisional.
Converting Stage_80224FDC from s32 return values 0/1 to bool with true/false made intent clearer without changing behavior.
The itKinoko_ItemVars Vec3 conversion is a good example of recognizing adjacent scalar fields as vector data and updating call sites consistently.
decomp_lessons: For large item-table work, it is acceptable to temporarily consolidate tables in one compilation unit when the final split is unclear, but leave a clear follow-up because reviewers may notice the organization debt.

When item vars live in overlay-style regions like xDD4, encode offsets in comments and add narrowly named structs even when member names remain x0/x4; this improves matching and future reverse-engineering.
Replacing UNK prototypes with concrete signatures often requires cross-system header fixes in fighter, game-mode, stage, library, and collision code, not just item files.
If a callback may receive non-fighter victims or generic item/user data, widening a parameter to void* can be a matching tactic, but it should be documented by call-site evidence.
Vector recognition is valuable: converting groups of f32 fields into Vec2/Vec3 can clarify codegen and call semantics, as seen in Fighter Struct2074 and itKinoko_ItemVars.
Map-collision flag discoveries should be centralized in common_structs.h/mpcoll headers so item physics/collision code can share the same bit names.
Commenting out a whole file such as it_3F14.c can get a large matching PR landed, but it creates an explicit follow-up risk and should be revisited quickly.
naming_conventions: Item-specific variable structs use the pattern it<Name>_ItemVars, for example itKlap_ItemVars, itChicorita_ItemVars, itKinoko_ItemVars.
Item attribute structs use the pattern it<Name>Attr or it_<address-ish>_DatAttrs, for example itChicoritaAttr, itChicoritaLeafAttr, it_279D_DatAttrs, it_27B5_DatAttrs.
Unidentified item structs keep address-derived names such as it_279D_ItemVars and it_27B5_ItemVars until better semantic names are known.
Item variable members remain x0/x4/x60 etc. with offset comments such as ip+DD4 and ip+E34 when semantics are uncertain.
New item command callback type is named ItCmd and takes Item_GObj* plus FtCmdState*.
Collision helpers introduced or exposed use the itColl_ prefix, for example itColl_BounceOffVictim and itColl_BounceOffShield.
Map collision flags follow MPCOLL_FLAGS_BNN naming for unknown bit flags, with semantic names like MPCOLL_CEIL and MPCOLL_GRPUSH retained where known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used helper inlines in item core code, including it_8026E_inline and it_8026EECC_inline_* helpers, likely to control repeated code shape for matching.
Preserved static data with address-like names such as it_803B8560, it_803B8570, it_803B85A8, it_803F1418, and it_803F14C4 rather than inventing unsupported semantic names.
Replaced scalar/padding regions with exact-size typed structs where field access indicated Vec2/Vec3/u32/u16 layout.
Kept uncertain constants and data commented in place, for example commented float/static data in it_266F.c and comments like 'timer?', 'scale?', and 'Floor??'.
Used concrete bool returns and true/false literals in simple 0/1 functions to clarify intent while preserving generated control flow.
Added or adjusted forward declarations/includes instead of broad concrete includes where possible, such as it/forward.h additions for Item_GObj and ItCmd.

review_feedback.feedback: This is a lot of nice work. Not sure why commenting out it_3F14 is necessary but it can be fixed later.

review_feedback.author: AaronB-Hub
review_feedback.feedback: The author said they were not sure how the item tables should be split across files, so they put them all together in one file for now.

follow_up_queries: Why was src/melee/it/it_3F14.c commented out in PR 1488, and was its content later restored or split into item table files?
Which later PR split the consolidated item tables from it_2725.c into their final files?
Did the TODO about applying a Struct2074-like typed layout to item->xD94 through xDA8 get resolved?
Are MPCOLL_UNK, MPCOLL_FLAGS_B20, and MPCOLL_FLAGS_B21 later given semantic names?
Did itChicoritaAttr and itChicoritaLeafAttr member names get refined beyond x4/x8/xC/x10?
Was ftColl_80076808's void* victim parameter later narrowed to a concrete shared type?
